change_summary: detail_verdict_text expanded 14.7× (64 → 942 chars) since Cycle 2. Added a second paragraph invoking the Part 3.1b 'trivial-transcription test' and the dome author's own timestamp definition. Verdict field unchanged ('Std Model Explains'). Claim, finding, tldr_evidence, tldr_verdict all within tolerance since Cycle 2. Detail_evidence unchanged apart from minor tightening already captured in Cycle 2 fingerprint.
our_argument_summary: Our review keeps 'Std Model Explains'. The detail_evidence argues gravitational torque (Clairaut, perturbation theory, LLR-confirmed to mm) produces the 18.613-year period, so the dome's 'epicyclic gears' label adds no mechanism. Cycle 3 adds a second verdict paragraph explicitly tying WIN-020 to our Part 3.1b post-hoc framework: textbook value, no legitimate-novelty carve-out, dome author's own weaker timestamp test unverifiable because 'the hardcoded expected value sits in the same commit as the live fetch call.'

relevant_files: monitor.py (not present in raw-text dump; Cycles 1–2 confirmed no WIN-020 handler exists)
dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: No calculation pipeline exists for WIN-020. The only dome material for this WIN is a single sentence in 02-wins.txt: 'WIN-020 — Lunar Declination 18.6-Year Cycle CONFIRMED (epicyclic gears, no Newton).' No gear ratios, no driver, no period derivation, no monitor.py check.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: Neither. There is no static pred==obs check and no live computation. WIN-020 is metadata-only — an asserted 'CONFIRMED' label attached to a millennia-old observation.
data_sources_used: None — no data is fetched or compared for WIN-020
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: None — no computation exists to require escape logic.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: The new Cycle 3 detail_verdict_text asserts: 'the hardcoded expected value sits in the same commit as the live fetch call, making even that weaker test unverifiable.' This sentence describes WINs that HAVE monitor.py handlers (the same-commit-hardcoding pattern Part 3.1b documents). For WIN-020 specifically, monitoring='none' — there is no hardcoded expected value AND no live fetch call. Applying the same-commit critique to WIN-020 in its own verdict block is inaccurate. The sentence needs to be rewritten to describe WIN-020's actual structural posture ('metadata-only, no check of any kind'), or removed.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: Cycles 1 and 2 both established no monitor.py handler exists for WIN-020. Grepping the raw-text dump for 'lunar', 'nodal', '18.6', '18.613', and '020' in 02-wins.txt returns only the metadata line 'WIN-020 — Lunar Declination 18.6-Year Cycle CONFIRMED (epicyclic gears, no Newton).' No other raw-text file contains matching material. Per the tag definition, 'none' is correct.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: 18.613-year nodal regression is explained by solar gravitational torque on the Moon's inclined orbit (Clairaut 1749–52 derivation; modern perturbation theory). The dome keeps the same numerical period and renames the mechanism 'epicyclic gears' without producing a gear ratio or driver.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: The period is in undergraduate textbooks and was encoded mechanically ~100 BC (Antikythera). The dome adopted the known value and labelled it 'CONFIRMED' — the archetype of the Part 3.1b 'trivial-transcription' failure mode.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: No derivation from disc_radius, firmament_height, or any dome parameter exists anywhere in the dome content. 'Gears' is asserted as a label, not computed.

holes_found.description: Internal inconsistency in the new detail_verdict_text. The second paragraph applies Part 3.1b's 'smoking gun' — hardcoded expected value living in the same commit as the live data fetch — directly to WIN-020. But our own code_analysis.monitoring tag for WIN-020 is 'none': there is no fetch and no hardcoded check because monitor.py has no handler for this WIN at all. A careful dome defender will notice our verdict text asserts a same-commit-hardcoding structure that doesn't exist in WIN-020's case and flag it as rhetorical over-reach.
holes_found.recommendation: Rewrite the offending sentence to describe WIN-020's actual structural posture. Suggested replacement: 'For WIN-020 the weaker timestamp test is inapplicable in the opposite direction — there is no monitor.py handler, no hardcoded expected value, and no live fetch. The period appears only as a metadata entry attached to an observation that has been continuously measured for over two millennia.' This preserves the trivial-transcription argument (which does apply) while removing the incorrect same-commit assertion.

holes_found.description: Cycle 2 already flagged (minor hole) that 'Babylonian eclipse records' in detail_evidence conflates the Saros cycle (223 synodic months ≈ 18.030 years) with the nodal regression period (18.613 years). The detail_evidence was NOT updated in Cycle 3 — the conflation remains. With the verdict now explicitly invoking 'no legitimate-novelty carve-out,' precision about what exactly was historically measured matters more, because the Saros encoded in the Antikythera is eclipse prediction (driven by syzygy alignment), not direct nodal precession period measurement. A sharp critic could turn this into 'your own review conflates two 18-ish-year cycles.'
holes_found.recommendation: Either add a parenthetical: '(Antikythera tracked the closely related 18.03-year Saros via its 223-tooth eclipse dial; the 18.613-year nodal period was derived from first principles after Clairaut 1749).' Or drop the Babylonian reference and lean only on the Antikythera mechanism plus Clairaut.

holes_found.description: detail_evidence still says 'The dome's epicyclic gears provide no physical driver for the 18.613-year period and no mechanism to derive it from dome geometry.' This is true, but the new verdict paragraph's phrase 'the derivation (where one is offered at all) is algebraically equivalent to a standard-model derivation' implies at least SOME derivation is offered somewhere. The dome offers none for WIN-020 (this is established across Cycles 1–3). The 'where one is offered at all' hedge is therefore gratuitous here — it applies to other WINs (WIN-001 Tesla, WIN-013 κ) but not WIN-020.
holes_found.recommendation: Change 'the derivation (where one is offered at all) is algebraically equivalent to a standard-model derivation' to 'no dome-specific derivation is offered at all' for the WIN-020 verdict block. Save the 'where one is offered at all' wording for WINs that actually offer a derivation.

holes_found.description: The finding one-liner reads: 'Non-discriminating: period is an input (known for over 2,000 years, encoded in Antikythera mechanism ~100 BC); dome's gears have no physical driver or amplitude prediction.' This duplicates the 'known for over 2,000 years' plus Antikythera frame that the detail_evidence will repeat. Nothing wrong with the repetition per se, but the finding could pack more by adding the inclination/amplitude specifics Cycle 2 recommended (lunar inclination 5.145° to ecliptic, declination swing ±18.3° to ±28.6°) — a Cycle 2 minor-rec that appears not to have been applied.
holes_found.recommendation: Optional polish: 'Non-discriminating — period is an ancient observable (encoded in Antikythera mechanism ~100 BC); dome specifies no gear ratios, no driver, and no prediction for lunar inclination (5.145°) or the ±5° declination-extreme swing that the cycle actually modulates.' This carries Cycle 2's orbital-inclination recommendation forward.

stronger_arguments.description: Quantitative grip: note that gravitational-torque perturbation theory derives the nodal regression rate as roughly -5°/year (or the full 360°/18.6y = 19.34°/y), and this prediction now enters LLR station-keeping algorithms at mm accuracy. A dome 'gear' model that cannot produce a rate — let alone an accelerating/decelerating rate over an eclipse season — fails the discriminatory test not just philosophically but numerically.
sources: Dickey et al. (1994) Lunar Laser Ranging: A Continuing Legacy of the Apollo Program, Science 265:482
Murphy (2013) Lunar laser ranging: the millimeter challenge, Rept. Prog. Phys. 76:076901

stronger_arguments.description: The dome's 'no Newton' parenthetical is stronger to attack directly: Newton's Principia derivation of lunar nodal regression got roughly half the correct value, Clairaut's 1749 resolution via higher-order perturbation terms landed at 18.6 years, and it was precisely the Newton-to-Clairaut progression that cemented universal gravitation. The dome doesn't 'replace Newton,' it simply declines the derivation challenge. Making this explicit in the verdict block pre-empts the defender move 'standard physics still needs Newton, which isn't proven outside a spherical Earth.'
sources: Wilson (1980) Perturbations and solar tables from Lacaille to Delambre, Archive for History of Exact Sciences 22:53–188

kernel_of_truth.description: Mechanical/gear reproduction of the 18.6-year cycle is genuinely possible — the Antikythera mechanism demonstrated exactly that kind of astronomical gearing. The cycle is also a real, physically consequential modulation (tidal heights, lunar standstill, even subtle climate forcing). So the dome is right that 18.6 years is a crisp, measurable number worth any cosmology's attention.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Using gears as a metaphor for a mechanism is not the same as specifying one. The Antikythera's gear ratios are computable from tooth counts; the dome's 'epicyclic gears' have no teeth, no drive, and no ratios. Without those, 'gears' is a placeholder word for the very thing standard physics already derives (solar gravitational torque on an inclined orbit). The predicted value is identical in both accounts — but only one account actually predicts it.

deeper_analysis:
The Cycle 3 verdict expansion is a structural-argument upgrade: it glues WIN-020 to our Part 3.1b framework so readers see it as one of the trivial-transcription archetypes rather than a standalone scientific objection. That move is good; the phrase 'textbook physics (Part 3.1b, trivial-transcription test)' is on-target, and 'no legitimate-novelty carve-out applies' is the correct Part 3.1b carve-out test.

The bug is paragraph surgery. The 'smoking gun' sentence from Part 3.1b — 'hardcoded expected value sits in the same commit as the live fetch call' — was copy-pasted into the WIN-020 block without checking whether WIN-020 actually has a live fetch. It doesn't (monitoring='none'). The analogous sentence for WIN-020 should describe its actual structural posture: no handler at all. Fixing this sentence is a one-line edit and prevents a dome defender who reads Part 3.1b alongside WIN-020 from saying 'you're describing infrastructure that isn't there.'

The Saros/nodal conflation is a Cycle 2 unfixed minor hole, upgraded to moderate here because the new verdict text invokes 'no legitimate-novelty carve-out applies.' If we claim rigorous application of our own Part 3.1b test, we should be rigorous about what ancient civilizations actually measured vs predicted. The distinction is small but a careful reader will catch it.

Advocate Mode (Lens A): the strongest defender rebuttal under our methodological principles is the 'early-model latitude' argument: 'Your internal-consistency principle cuts both ways. The dome model is a young framework; standard physics took Newton→Clairaut→LLR, centuries of refinement, to derive 18.613 years to mm precision. Judging a fledgling geometric model by the same bar is the default-favoritism you claim to avoid.' This rates ~3/5: superficially appealing, but our own text already preempts it implicitly by noting the dome labels WIN-020 'CONFIRMED' (not 'open' or 'work in progress'). Making that explicit in the verdict — 'the dome itself promotes this to CONFIRMED, not pending-refinement' — would tighten the preempt. Rated 3 → recommendation: add a sentence noting the dome's 'CONFIRMED' label is the dome author's own stated bar, not ours.

Cross-WIN consistency (Lens B): checked against WIN-021 (Lunar/Earth size ratio), WIN-022 (Lunar Libration), WIN-037 (Lunar orbital period 27.3d). In all four, our argument is 'textbook value, no dome derivation.' Compatible. No cross-contradictions detected. One style note: the four WINs use slightly different phrasings for the same critique (WIN-020 'no physical driver,' WIN-037 'standard Kepler,' WIN-021 'angular diameter identity') — a stylistic harmonization pass across lunar WINs would tighten the narrative but isn't a review-blocker.

Quantitative verification (Lens C): 18.613 Julian years = 6798.38 days is the standard nodal regression period (I computed 6798.383/365.25 = 18.6130). Our text's '18.613' is correct to 4 significant figures. Saros = 223 × 29.53059 = 18.030 years (distinct from nodal period, supports the moderate hole). Lunar inclination 5.145° and declination extremes ±18.3° to ±28.6° check out. No numerical errors in the prose.

citations_verified: Nature 444:587 (2006) — Freeth et al., Antikythera mechanism: DOI resolves, paper describes the gear train and astronomical functions including the Saros-based eclipse dial.
citations_unchecked: LLR millimeter-precision claim — consistent with Murphy 2013 RPP and APOLLO public results but not cited inline. Not a failure; a specificity opportunity if we want to strengthen.

advocate_mode.best_defense: The dome model's 'epicyclic gears' language is a placeholder for a mechanism that any cosmology must eventually supply, and judging a young geometric framework against LLR millimeter precision — developed over 275 years of standard-physics refinement — violates this review's own 'no default favoritism' principle. The 18.6-year period is a physical observable both frameworks must accommodate; at worst, WIN-020 is an open item for the dome, not a disproof. Your own Part 3.1b rule explicitly carves out 'legitimate novelty' — the dome's contribution is precisely the geometric reframing, and dismissing it before a dome-community mathematical physics effort can produce gear ratios is the same bar you reject for the standard model.
advocate_mode.preemptive_recommendation: Add a short clause to the verdict: 'WIN-020 fails the trivial-transcription test under the dome's own CONFIRMED label — not under a pending-refinement label the dome declined to use.' This anchors our critique to the dome author's chosen status, not to an externally imposed maturity bar, and neutralizes the 'give the young model time' rebuttal.

quantitative_verification.computations: Nodal regression period 6798.383 days / 365.25 = 18.6130 years (matches our text to 4 sig figs). Saros = 223 × 29.53059 days / 365.25 = 18.030 years (distinct, supporting moderate hole). Max/min declination = 23.44° obliquity ± 5.145° inclination = 28.585° / 18.295° (matches standard lunar standstill bounds).
